name: Japanese Tea Garden
summary_en: Archive documents span from a 1946 Recreation summer program calling it the oldest public Japanese tea garden to 1980 commission minutes reporting on its admission-fee revenue. Planning environmental impact reports from 2000-2002 for the new de Young Museum and the Golden Gate Park Concourse Authority reference the garden and its surroundings, and a long run of 'What's doing in the parks' bulletins (1978-1991) advertise daily garden tours. The Japanese Tea Garden is operated by the Recreation and Park Department, whose FY2025 budget line is about $250M. No contract in the data names it.
